title: gypsophila umbricola
intro: Gypsophila umbricola, also known as mountain gypsophila, is a low-growing, spreading perennial plant in the family Caryophyllaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Gypsophila umbricola are said to include reducing fever, relieving headaches, and soothing sore throats, as well as acting as a diuretic to aid in the treatment of urinary tract infections. Gypsophila umbricola can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used in traditional herbal remedies.
